characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Thelma is the matriarch of the Harper family, with most of the show focusing on her and her relationships to the rest of her family. Adaptational Nice Guy: Mama is much nicer here than in the "Family" sketches, though still salty and cranky. Age Insecurity: During the final round of the Grandma U.S.A. pageant, Thelma gets infuriated when the host asks her how old she is and tells him its none of his damn business. Apron Matron: She's a stern, elderly woman who has little patience for her family's antics andeven less for anyone that would cross them. Berserk Button: One episode revealed that underage drinking is a HUGE berserk button for Mama due to an incident involving Eunice years earlier.

characters.name: Thelma "Mama" Harper
characters.description: Thelma is the matriarch of the Harper family, with most of the show focusing on her and her relationships to the rest of her family. Adaptational Nice Guy: Mama is much nicer here than in the "Family" sketches, though still salty and cranky. Age Insecurity: During the final round of the Grandma U.S.A. pageant, Thelma gets infuriated when the host asks her how old she is and tells him its none of his damn business. Apron Matron: She's a stern, elderly woman who has little patience for her family's antics andeven less for anyone that would cross them. Berserk Button: One episode revealed that underage drinking is a HUGE berserk button for Mama due to an incident involving Eunice years earlier.

characters.name: Vinton "Vint" Harper
characters.description: Vinton is Thelma's adult son. He moves in with her at the start of the series after his first wife leaves him and remains in her household for the duration of the series. Acquired Situational Narcissism: "Amateur Night" sees Vint winning a talent contest at the Bigger Jigger with hisFred Astaireimpression. His ego almost instantly swells to enormous proportions, to the point where he decides to ditch the impression and perform a whole new routine as the flashy "Vinnie Vegas". Mama talks him out of making an ass of himself at the last minute. Beware the Nice Ones: While he's normally a very mild-mannered man, "Harper Versus Harper" ends with him losing his temper with Thelma and Naomi after being dragged into the middle of their courtroom dispute, cowing both of them into silence. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Vinton's never been the sharpest knife in the drawer, but he takes his job as a locksmith very seriously and has a surprising amount of knowledge about locks and keys. Catchphrase: A sarcastic "Thanks a lot, Mama!".

characters.name: Naomi Harper
characters.description: Naomi starts the series as one of Thelma's neighbors, though she soon marries Vinton and moves into Thelma's basement. Affectionate Nickname: "Skeeter", as called by Vinton. Betty and Veronica: The Veronica to Iola's Betty, being a flirtatious blonde with anextensive list of exesbefore settling down with Vint. Color-Coded Characters: Naomi's wardrobe seemingly consists solely of yellow sundresses, especially after the switch to syndication. Cordon Bleugh Chef: Her famous "international stew" consists of canned stew, canned peas, canned Mexican corn, canned sauerkraut, and Spaghetti-O's.

characters.name: Fran Crawley
characters.description: Thelma's spinster sister and an aspiring writer. She dies prior to the start of season 3 and is largely forgotten for the rest of the series. Cannot Keep a Secret: She admits to this when Naomi asks if she can confide in her. Later that same episode, Thelma is able to get her to spill her guts just from forcing Fran to look her in the eye. Giftedly Bad: She considers herself to be a superb journalist, although most of her articles end up being heavily edited (usually leaving just the things that other people contributed to the article). Old Maid: She remained unmarried up until her death. Thelma would occasionally refer to her as one during their arguments. Secretly Wealthy: Downplayed; after her death, she had enough money saved up to leave Thelma $35,000 even after making a large donation to her favorite charity.

characters.name: Sonja Harper
characters.description: Vinton's teenage daughter. She moves into Thelma's house with him, though she inexplicably leaves before season 3 and is never mentioned again. Bratty Teenage Daughter: Especially in the first season, where she was moody, whiny, and almost always bopping around the house to music on her headphones,oblivious to the rest of the family. She mellowed out considerably in season two,which had the unintended consequence of making her as vanilla as her brother. Missing Mom: Her mother unexpectedly left the family behind to become a cocktail waitress in Las Vegas just before the start of the series. Naomi later becomes the mother figure in her life after Vinton remarries. Put on a Bus: She is never seen after syndication, and is only mentioned once afterward (specifically about her absence from Fran's funeral). It's never explained where she or Buzz went, either; some sources say they went to live with their mother in Las Vegas, while others say they went to college. Tomboy and Girly Girl: The Tomboy to Naomi's Girly Girl.

characters.name: Buzz Harper
characters.description: Vinton's teenage son. He moves into Thelma's house with him, though he inexplicably leaves before season 3 and is never mentioned again The Dog Bites Back: After his Homecoming date dumps him for wearing a clip-on tie, Buzz takes the spark plug out of the car belonging to the guy she left him for. Vintproudly declares, "That's my boy!" Flat Character: Buzz is quite possibly the blandest recurring character in the entire series, with most one-shot characters showing greater depth and relevance to the plot than he ever did. Missing Mom: His mother unexpectedly left the family behind to become a cocktail waitress in Las Vegas just before the start of the series. Naomi later becomes the mother figure in his life after Vinton remarries, though their interactions are limited. Only Known by Their Nickname: His real name is Vinton Harper, Jr., though this is only mentioned once in the entire series.

characters.name: Bubba Higgins
characters.description: Eunice and Ed's son and Thelma's grandson. He debuts in season 3, fresh out of juvenile hall, and moves into Thelma's house due to his parents moving to Florida and his probation preventing him from leaving the state. Big Eater: His debut episode has the Harper family returning home after Fran's funeral only to find that Bubba has eaten almost the entire buffet laid out for Fran's wake. Burger Fool: He gets a job as a cashier at a fast-food restaurant. The Casanova: Bubba seemingly has a different girlfriend on a near-weekly basis. Character Development: In his early appearances, Bubba was a boorish juvenile delinquent. He later develops into a friendlier, more ambitious and obedient teenager (thanks to Mama's influence) and is considerably more mature by the time he enters college (though no less goofy).

characters.name: Iola Boylen
characters.description: Thelma's spinster neighbor, who harbors an unrequited crush on Vinton, much to Naomi's ire. A-Cup Angst: Implied in several episodes. Canon Foreigner: Iola didn't debut until Season 3 (the start of the series' syndicated run), despite being portrayed as having lived across the street from the Harpers her whole life. Color-Coded Characters: She wears pink dresses (with a handful exceptions) in virtually every appearance. Crappy Homemade Gift: Her homemade gifts are usually treated as such by the Harpers. One episode involves the Harper family trying to sell off all of her gifts in a garage sale, only for no one to show any interest even when Thelma lowers the prices to practically nothing.

characters.name: Ellen Harper-Jackson
characters.description: Thelma's oldest daughter. Deadpan Snarker: Ellen can be every bit as snarky as her mother at times. It's All About Me: According to Thelma, Ellen always had to be the first for everything as a child. May–December Romance: She dates a much younger man in "Ellen's Boyfriend", much to Thelma's disapproval. Put on a Bus: She only appears in one episode after the show's switch to syndication. Unlike Ed and Eunice (who are stated to have moved to Florida), no explanation is given for her continued absence and she is only mentioned a handful of times for the rest of the series.

characters.name: Eunice Higgins
characters.description: Thelma's younger daughter and Bubba's mother. Big Sister Bully: When they were kids, she forced Vinton to dress up as Tinkerbell for Halloween to match her Peter Pan costume. Bratty Teenage Daughter: As seen in "Sins of the Mother". Demoted to Extra: Eunice started out as the main character of the "Family" sketches that the series was based on. Once the series took off, she was reduced to a recurring character for the first two seasons before beingPut on a Buswhen Carol Burnett declined to return for season three. Drama Queen: And how!

characters.name: Ed Higgins
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: Ed's role is greatly reduced from what it was in the "Family" sketches, only appearing in a handful of episodes before beingPut on a Busto Florida when Harvey Korman declined to return for season three. Henpecked Husband: It comes with being married to Eunice. Hypocritical Humor: He takes offense to Eunice declaring that the only man she ever loved was Duke Reeves, but that same episode shows that he is on a first name basis with the prostitute that Thelma and Eunice meet in jail. Put on a Bus: He and Eunice move to Florida for the syndicated run, due to Harvey Korman declining to return for Season 3.

characters.name: Aunt Effie Harper
characters.description: Thelma's sister-in-law and Vinton's aunt. Ascended Extra: She was originally intended to be a one-shot character for Vint and Naomi's wedding. She proved so popular that she became a recurring character during the syndicated run. Embarrassing Hospital Gown: After being hospitalized with a concussion, Aunt Effie wanders out of her room and is found by Thelma in the hospital gift shop with the back of her hospital gown wide open. Playing Sick: She fakes having a fall to get Thelma to come do chores for her in "No Room at the Inn".

characters.name: Uncle Roy Harper
characters.description: Thelma's brother-in-law and Vinton's uncle. The Charmer: With the exception of Mama, the whole family quickly warms up to Roy. Even Mama comes around in the end. Cool Old Guy: His various anecdotes allude to a colorful and adventurous life, a stark contrast to the mundanity of the Harper household. Cool Uncle: Vinton and Ellen are absolutely ecstatic to see him when he suddenly drops in for the first time in decades. Locked Out of the Loop: Due to the Harpers not having any way of contacting him, he doesn't find out about his brother Carl's death until 11 years after the fact.

characters.name: Carl Harper
characters.description: Thelma's late husband. Guys are Slobs: Thelma describes him as "a bum in a dirty t-shirt". His sole on-screen appearance is every bit as slovenly as advertised. Lazy Husband: Carl willingly passes up on a promotion at work with little regards to his family's financial situation, and can't be bothered to get off his ass when Thelma is in labor with their first child. A Man Is Always Eager: Inverted; Thelma frequently implies that Carl was borderline asexual when he was alive.Thelma: How did I end up raising such a pack of sex maniacs? (Beat) Well, they sure didn't get it from their daddy. Posthumous Character: Carl's been dead for over a decade when the series starts.

characters.name: Reverend Lloyd Meachum
characters.description: The preacher at Mama's church. Beware the Nice Ones: He's consistently portrayed as one of the nicest characters on the show, but even he has his limits, as his grandson Eugene learns the hard way at the end of "Child's Play". Good Shepherd: He's generally shown to be a friendly, mild-mannered minister even when dealing with the Harpers' absurdity. When Thelma's "foreign exchange student" is revealed to be an escapee from juvenile hall, he says nothing initially and praises Thelma and the other Harpers for defending the girl from Alberta and convincing her to go back to serve out the rest of her sentence, appointing Thelma as president of the Church Ladies' League. Henpecked Husband: Strongly implied to be the case. Pirates Who Don't Do Anything: Downplayed; he is never seen inside his church, although he still seen performing his duties in other settings, such as Vint and Naomi's wedding (held at Mama's house) or funerals.

characters.name: Alberta Meachum
characters.description: Reverend Meachum's wife and Mama's rival in the Church Ladies' League. The Kingmaker: When Thelma tries to run for an unprecedented second term as Church Ladies' League president, Alberta nominates Iola to run against her, turning the two friends against one another. The Rival: To Thelma within the Church Ladies' League.

characters.name: Mayor Alvin Tutweiler
characters.description: The mayor of Raytown and Ellen's lover. The Bus Came Back: After not appearing since the second season, he reappears in the season 5 episode "What a Dump" to announce that the Harpers' neighborhood has been chosen as the sight of the city's new landfill. Mayor Pain: Thelma accuses him of being the incompetent variety, though it is ultimately subverted when he is shown to have enough political skill to help Thelma resolve all of her issues during her brief tenure as mayor. Vetinari Job Security: When Thelma beats him in the election, her tenure as mayor is nothing short of disastrous, and at Ellen's insistence, he steps in to offer her advice to smooth things out. Afterwards, Thelma resigns from office and they use an obscure law in Raytown's charter to have Tutweiler reinstated as mayor.

characters.name: Mr. Hanson
characters.description: Thelma and Bubba's night-school teacher, as well as Thelma's love interest. Teacher/Student Romance: He and Thelma strike up a romantic relationship while he is still her remedial English teacher.

characters.name: Claude Canemaker
characters.description: Vint's friend in several early episodes. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Claude only appears in a few episodes in the first season before disappearing from the show altogether, never to be mentioned again. Toxic Friend Influence: His few appearances have him using his friendship to manipulate Vint into doing some morally (and legally) questionable things, such as trying to marry Vint to a Portugese womanso she can get a green cardor pawning off Mama's silver to bail Claude out of jail.
